_comment: Phase 5 + v0.7.0 joinable NPC templates. Each template defines a 'rarity' (common, uncommon, rare, legendary) and an optional weight for the spawn roll. min_player_level is the absolute minimum the player must be at. min_faction_rep is per-faction (null = any faction). requires_quest is an optional mission id that must be in the player's active missions. tier is the armor/weapon tier the NPC starts with. role is one of the roles in npc_name_parts.titles. v0.7.0 added preferred_biomes (null = universal) and a per-biome _biome_themes section for flavor names/titles.

templates.title: wanderer
templates.description: A drifter from outside the local area. Easy to recruit early on.

templates.title: mercenary
templates.description: An officer of one of the factions. Better gear, higher requirements.

templates.title: shaman
templates.description: Locked behind a quest. Powerful specialist.

templates.title: wanderer
templates.description: Legendary NPCs with their own backstories. Open shops at your base if you treat them right.

templates._comment: v0.7.0: biome-specific templates. preferred_biomes is a list of biome names the template is native to. Templates without it (or with null) are universal.
templates.title: wastelander
templates.description: A scrappy survivor of the wastes. At home in any dry biome.

templates.title: bogs-runner
templates.description: A marsh-dweller who knows the bogs like the back of their hand.

templates.title: ironwood-runner
templates.description: An agile forager who can navigate the metallic forest.

templates.title: storm-chaser
templates.description: A weather-hardened surveyor of the highlands.

templates.title: corpse-collector
templates.description: A grim forager who turns the dead into useful resources.

templates.title: city-explorer
templates.description: A scavenger who knows the dead city's secrets.

templates.title: rust-prospector
templates.description: A scrapper who finds value in corroded metal.

templates._comment: v0.7.0 faction-specific templates. preferred_factions restricts the template to settlements owned by one of the listed factions. Templates without preferred_factions (null) are universal across factions.
templates.title: pact-guard
templates.description: A veteran enforcer of the Iron Accord. Patrols the perimeter of Pact settlements.

templates.title: hollow-warden
templates.description: A ritual-bound guardian of the Hollow Covenant. Wards the boundary between surface and Underearth.

templates.title: serpent-raider
templates.description: A mounted raider of the Ash Serpents. Knows the wasteland routes like the back of their hand.

templates.title: veil-warden
templates.description: A silent guardian of the Veilwardens. Keeps forbidden knowledge sealed.

templates.title: choir-techie
templates.description: A Neon Choir acolyte, half-machine, half-zealot. Speaks in glitchy mantras.

templates.title: bone-broker
templates.description: A Bone Circuit broker. Trades in parts you can't find on any legitimate market.

templates.title: caravan-guard
templates.description: A Last Caravans guard. Protects the trade routes, armed to the teeth.

_spawn_rules._comment: Per-hex-visit spawn rules. Most hexes have no joinable NPCs (the spawn rate is low).

_biome_themes._comment: v0.7.0: per-biome flavor for procedural NPC generation. The 'title' is used as a role prefix; the 'name_prefix' is added before the randomly generated surname for flavor. The 'origin_pref' shifts the race origin bias (upworld vs underworld) for this biome.

_faction_themes._comment: v0.7.0: per-faction flavor for procedural NPC generation in faction-owned settlements. The 'name_prefix' is added before the randomly generated surname. The 'race_pref' shifts the race origin bias. The 'preferred_race' directly sets the race (human, chthon, vespers, nullborn, etc.). Faction themes OVERRIDE biome themes for the name_prefix and race_pref (faction identity is stronger than biome identity).
